danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile characters (zombies, thugs, police) with distinct sprites
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player progression; combat/avoidance required

danger_signals.visual_form: Spikes, pits, environmental obstacles with contrasting visual design
danger_signals.meaning: Collision damage; instant fail zones in platforming segments

danger_signals.visual_form: Large, uniquely animated sprite with distinct color palette
danger_signals.meaning: Major world-state barrier; progression blocked until defeated

safety_signals.visual_form: Glowing items, icons with bright coloration
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability or enhanced abilities; player empowerment

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear platform geometry, unobstructed walkways with visual continuity
Character sprites with distinct appearance, visual separation from enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Mission objective completion; safe zone indicator

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Iconic silhouette pose; humanoid character sprite with musical association
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dance floors, performance stages, music-themed level aesthetics

faction_indicators.color_identity: Varied (zombies green/gray, thugs darker, police uniform colors)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct enemy sprite designs; no unified faction symbol
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types per level with different movement patterns and attack behaviors
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban/industrial zones; abandoned areas where enemies cluster

faction_indicators.color_identity: Bright, contrasting colors; distinct from enemy palette
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Confined spaces, rooms requiring navigation; often visually isolated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard enemy patterns; platform geometry fully visible; ambient lighting consistent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol or move in predictable patterns; player movement unimpeded

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level exit becomes accessible; visual transition toward next stage area
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies may cease spawning or change patrol patterns; player gains passage forward

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Arena becomes isolated; boss sprite dominates screen space; unique visual styling for boss character
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss executes multi-phase attack patterns; player restricted to arena; faster enemy movement/attacks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player sprite may change appearance or display visual effect overlay; screen effects/animation changes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player invulnerability or enhanced attack capability; enemies take damage on contact; changed interaction rules

environmental_storytelling.example: Different levels present thematically distinct environments (urban, industrial, fantastical) communicating progression through Michael Jackson's world
Specific enemy types appearing in thematic locations (e.g., zombies in spooky areas) reinforcing stage atmosphere and danger context
Children positioned in visible but obstructed locations communicates rescue mission structure; their visual vulnerability reinforces stakes

environmental_storytelling.technique: Level exit gating through visual lockdown
environmental_storytelling.example: Forward passage blocked by visual/geometric barriers until rescue objective completed; world-state tied to mission completion

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Slow, shambling movement; predictable directional paths
Movement speed increases; may lurch toward player position
Direct approach toward player; attack execution with melee or projectile patterns
Faster movement than zombies; coordinated patrol patterns suggesting organized opposition
Movement speed may increase; enemies flee or take damage on contact; visual invulnerability indicator active
Basic attack patterns; movement telegraphed through animation
Attack frequency increases; movement becomes erratic; visual intensity escalates

notes: Michael Jackson's Moonwalker uses straightforward visual communication for a 1990 platformer. Danger is communicated through enemy sprite design and environmental hazards. Faction identity relies on color palette differentiation rather than symbolic markers. World-state changes manifest primarily through enemy behavior modification and level geometry access. The rescue mission structure (collecting children) creates a clear objective visualization system. Power-up states provide positive feedback through visual effect layering. Limited source material available for this T2 extraction.
